items.title: Billions of logins stolen in multiple data hacks over time now leaked in massive breach | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Researchers from CyberNews have detected a massive global cyber breach. According to cyber security researchers, billions of login credentials stolen in multiple data-breach incidents over time were accessed by various 'infostealer malware', compiled into datasets, then leaked online. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Login details involving Facebook and Google credentials leaked
0:36 Ryan Kalember, cyber security expert, on details of cyber breach and how users can protect themselves

items.title: Defense analyst: Israel-Iran war 'first truly long-range fire war in modern warfare' | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The war of attrition between Israel and Iran may come down to a battle of numbers. Military analysts have suggested that Iran may be rapidly running out of missiles, while Israel is burning through its stock of interceptor missiles used to shoot incoming missiles down. How much longer can each side carry on with their current military stock?

Chapter Breakdown:
0:00 Iran and Israel's missile stock
3:08 Farzin Nadimi, Security Analyst, Senior Fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, on Iran's missile stockpiles and Israel's domination of Iranian airspace
10:01 Burcu Ozcelik, Middle East Security Analyst, Royal United Services Institute, on prospects for Iran negotiations while Israeli strikes on Iran continue


#longrangemissiles #israeliranwar #irannuclearprogram

items.title: Iranian state TV airs footage of what it says is aftermath of Israeli strikes in Isfahan province
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Iranian state TV showed images of what it says was the aftermath of Israeli strikes on a vehicle inspection center in Isfahan province. The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) also confirmed on Saturday that a separate location in Isfahan — a centrifuge manufacturing workshop at Iran's Isfahan nuclear site — had been hit. The IAEA chief, Rafael Grossi, said in a statement:
"We know this facility well. There was no nuclear material at this site and therefore the attack on it will have no radiological consequences." 
Israel’s military says its attacks in Isfahan province were on a nuclear research facility and that they killed several senior Iranian commanders.

#dwnewsshorts #isfahan #israeliranwar 
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Belarusian opposition leader Siarhei Tsikhanouski released from jail after US envoy visit | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Siarhei Tsikhanouski, one of the leaders of Belarus’ opposition movement, has been released from jail. A total of 14 Belarusian political prisoners have been released. Tsikhanouski spent nearly five years behind bars due to his opposition to Belarusian leader Alexander Lukashenko. He was met by his wife Sviatlana on his release from jail. The surprise move came hours after Lukashenko met Donald Trump’s special envoy Keith Kellogg in Minsk.

#tsikhanouski
#belarus #keithkellogg


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: State of emergency: What's behind the pension protests in Panama's northwestern province? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Panama has declared a state of emergency in a poor, western province, after weeks of protests over pension reforms which would require workers to pay higher contributions for fewer benefits. In response to the pension strikes, Panama’s Congress enacted a new law in the banana sector last week designed to protect worker’s benefits such as medical assistance. On Thursday and Friday, rioters and masked individuals - presumably not related to the pension reform movement -  forced their way into Changuinola airport, set fire to a local baseball stadium, and damaged the closed Chiquita factory, leading to the government’s decision to temporarily suspend constitutional protections in the province. The recently declared state of emergency allows police to make arrests without a warrant and greatly restricts freedom of movement. 

Chapter Breakdown:
0:00 Protesters impose roadblocks in month-long protests against protest reforms in Panama's banana sector
1:37 Nivia Castrellon, Former Deputy Foreign Minister of Panama, on whether the government's response to protests has been proportionate


#chiquita #banana #panama 


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Netanyahu's gov't is the 'greatest obstacle to regional peace' says Erdogan at Islamic bloc meeting
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan urged Muslim countries to unite around Iran's right to defend itself and criticized Israel for starting this war. The Turkish leader's statement came as foreign ministers from the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) met in Istanbul on Saturday to discuss the war between Israel and Iran. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi also attended the meeting and warned that any US intervention would be dangerous for all parties. On Friday, Araghchi met with his counterparts of the UK, France, Germany, and the EU in Geneva, where he insisted that Iran would not engage in dialogue on its nuclear enrichment program until Israel were to cease its strikes on Iran. The OIC is comprised of 57 states, 49 of which have a majority Muslim population. 

#oic #israeliranwar #turkey 


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Pro-Palestinian activists break into British Royal Air Force base | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Footage posted online by Palestine Action on Friday shows two people inside the Oxfordshire airbase in darkness, with one riding a scooter up to an Airbus Voyager air-to-air refuelling tanker and appearing to spray paint into its jet engine.
 
In a statement, a Palestine Action spokesperson said: "Despite publicly condemning the Israeli government, Britain continues to send military cargo, fly spy planes over Gaza, and refuel U.S. and Israeli fighter jets."
 
There have been reports confirming that the RAF has flown over 500 surveillance missions over Gaza between 2023 and 2025

items.title: UNICEF warns children in Gaza could die of thirst | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Officials in Gaza say at least 44 people were killed by Israeli fire on Friday. Many of them had been trying to get food. Their deaths add to the hundreds of Palestinians killed in incidents near aid distribution sites since late May, when the Gaza Humanitarian Foundation bypassed the UN as the main supplier of aid in the Strip. The foundation is a US- and Israeli-backed entity recently created to feed the territory. As famine looms across Gaza after more than 20 months of war, UNICEF, the UN's children's agency, is now warning that thousands of children could die of thirst as water systems are collapsing. 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What's fueling Nigeria's drug epidemic? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Drug abuse in Nigeria ranks among the highest worldwide. What is driving it? DW's senior international correspondent Fanny Facsar and her team secretly filmed how one type of drug has become especially popular among Nigerian youth and is sold at nearly every street corner in Lagos: It is called "Colos" or Colorado, a mix of marijuana and chemicals. Experts warn of a drug epidemic but lament that other policy issues, namely Nigeria's dire economic and security situation, cause the crisis to be overlooked. Street dealers claim that the police is often involved in the business. Sources who work within Nigeria's drug federal agency, NDLEA, have told DW that they are not aware of police taking bribes. They also said they lack enough basic equipment and human resources to carry out more raids against drug networks.

00:00 Nigeria's drug epidemic
05:17 DW speaks with Dr Dokun Adedeji, who runs the Compassionate Care Recovery Initiative, a rehabilitation center in Lagos for substance abusers

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Regime change or unity: How Iranians feel about the conflict with Israel | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Fighting between Iran and Israel shows no sign of easing. Iranian media says there have been Israeli strikes against the Isfahan nuclear site in the central part of the country, though they report that no hazardous leaks have been detected. Hours earlier, an Iranian missile hit the Israeli city of Haifa, injuring 23 people. With the conflict now in its second week, Israel says there have been 25 deaths on its side. And while Tehran has not updated its death toll, a US-based human-rights group estimates 650 people may have been killed in Iran.
Iran's foreign minister has said that Tehran is ready to engage in diplomacy over its nuclear program, but not while Israel's offensive continues. He made the comment after talks with top European diplomats in Geneva, aimed at deescalating the conflict. The meeting ended without any concrete breakthroughs, but foreign ministers from France, the UK and Germany say they want to see further talks with Tehran.

00:00 Iran and Israel trade strikes as death toll mounts
03:02 DW's Emily Gordine reports from Tel Aviv
06:15 Iran says no to negotiations while under attack
06:57 Niloofar Gholami from DW's Persian Service provides analysis


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
